items.label: storefront-dev

shellCommands.command: cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/commerce-storefront && cat in-progress/product-quick-view_STATE.json 2>/dev/null || echo "No state file"

cd /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/commerce-storefront && npm start & ↵ SERVER_PID=$! ↵ echo "Server PID: $SERVER_PID" ↵ sleep 70 ↵ HTTP_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:3000
PLP_STATUS=$(curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:3000/category/newarrivals --max-time 30) ↵ echo "PLP route status: $PLP_STATUS" ↵ # Also check search ↵ SEARCH_STATUS=$(curl -s -o

# Kill any running dev server ↵ pkill -f "pwa-kit-dev" 2>/dev/null || true ↵ sleep 2 ↵ # SSR Safety: Check for window/document access outside guards ↵ echo "=== SSR Safety: window/document access ==="

# SSR Safety: Check for window/document access outside guards ↵ echo "=== SSR Safety: window/document access ===" ↵ grep -n 'window\|document' /workspaces/DAGent-t/apps/commerce-storefront/overrides/a
cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && git merge-base --is-ancestor d012578 HEAD && echo "YES: d012578 is ancestor of HEAD" || echo "NO: d012578 is NOT ancestor of HEAD"

cd /workspaces/DAGent-t && echo "=== Files in overrides ===" && find apps/commerce-storefront/overrides/app/components/product-tile apps/commerce-storefront/overrides/app/components/quick-view-modal -

messages: Files already exist! This might be a retry/fix scenario. Let me check the current state of these files and understand what's broken.
Now let me check if there's a pending context or prior failure, and run the existing tests to see their status:
All 26 tests pass. Now let me check if there's a prior failure state and verify the dev server works:
The component files are already committed. Let me verify the implementation is solid by checking the dev server:
Let me check on the server status differently:
Root route returns 200. Let me check the PLP route:
All routes return HTTP 200. The implementation is working correctly. Let me clean up the server and verify the SSR safety checklist:
All SSR safety checks pass. Let me also check the validate-app.sh hook and see if it needs updating:

The validate-app.sh already covers PLP routes. This feature doesn't add new routes — it enhances existing product tiles on PLP pages with a Quick View modal.  Let me verify all implementation files are committed:
All implementation files are already committed and present on the current branch. Let me do a final summary verification:

Everything is verified and clean. Here's the summary of what's been validated:  **Implementation (all 4 files committed):** 1. `overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.jsx` — ProductTile override with Quick View overlay bar (slide-up on hover desktop, always visible mobile) 2. `overrides/app/components/quick-view-modal/index.jsx` — QuickViewModal using `useProductViewModal` hook + `ProductView` with spinner/error/loaded states 3. `overrides/app/components/product-tile/index.test.js` — 12 unit tests for tile overlay 4. `overrides/app/components/quick-view-modal/index.test.js` — 14 unit tests for modal  **Verification results:** - ✅ 26/26 unit tests pass (100% line coverage) - ✅ Dev server HTTP 200 on `/` (home) - ✅ Dev server HTTP 200 on `/category/newarrivals` (PLP — affected route) - ✅ Dev server HTTP 200 on `/search?q=shirt` (search) - ✅ SSR safety: no `window`/`document` access, no `Date.now()`/`Math.random()`, no raw `fetch()` - ✅ All files already committed (commit `d012578`) - ✅ Working tree clean — no uncommitted changes

reportedOutcome.docNote: Product Quick View implemented via PWA Kit override pattern: ProductTile override wraps base tile with role="group" container and adds a full-width semi-transparent overlay bar (slide-up on desktop hover, always-visible on mobile) that opens a QuickViewModal. Modal reuses existing useProductViewModal hook and ProductView component with loading spinner, error state, and full product details including variant selectors and add-to-cart. Sets/bundles excluded. 26 unit tests, 100% line coverage.
